versions.version_description: I have included the full lyrics to celebrate her life and struggles., R.I.P Tina...

Powerful mid career pop-rock masterpiece from Tina here. Please follow the record to understand the pace of these changes as there are many passing chords and nuances. Tina’s phrasing really sets each section apart. Intro is a bit of a decrescendo falling into the first verse. D chord at the end of verse 1 is arpeggiated. The first pre-chorus sets up the arpeggio figure for most of the rest of the song, though this can be strummed or finger picked with a groove that feels good to you. The second line of interlude 2 is a decrescendo similar in feel to the intro figure. Bridge and refrain really contain a heavy funk influenced feel. The D and D6sus2 from refrain to chorus 3 are each held and really lift things up for the explosion about to happen. Outro plays as chorus 3 repeating to fade with many ad-libs.

tuning: E A D G B E

recommended.song_name: Whats Love Got To Do With It

content:
(M. Chapman, N. Chinn, H. Knight)

[Verse]

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
A prisoner of your love[/tab]
Entangled in your web
Hot whispers in the night
I'm captured by your spell - Captured!

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
Oh yes I'm touched by this show of emotion[/tab]
Should I be fractured by your lack of devotion
Should I, should I?
You better be good to me

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
That's how it's gotta be now[/tab]
'Cause I don't have no use
For what you loosely call the truth
And you better be good to me

[Verse]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
I think it's all so right[/tab]
That we don't need to fight
We stand face to face
And you present your case

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
Yes I know -- you keep telling me that you love me[/tab]
And I really do want to believe
But did you think I'd just accept you in blind faith
Oh sure babe, anything to please you
But you better be good to me

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                  [ch]D[/ch]
That's how it's gotta be now[/tab]
'Cause I don't have the time
For your over loaded lines
And you better be good to me
